QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_12

question.text: What is the minimum amount required to start investing in most mutual fund SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans)?

answer.text: As low as Rs. 500 per month

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Most mutual fund houses allow investors to start a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) with as little as Rs. 500 per month. Some even offer SIPs starting at Rs. 100. This low minimum makes mutual funds accessible to investors of all income levels. **The Trap:** Mutual funds do NOT require large sums to start investing. The misconception that you need lakhs of rupees to invest in mutual funds is wrong — Rs. 500 is enough to begin. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under SIP features. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The low entry amount is a game-changer when talking to retired clients with limited monthly income. Even someone with a small pension can start a SIP of Rs. 500-1,000 to build savings over time.
